01 / 03
Utilities Engineering—2023

Plant Hazard & Safety Studies

Detailed steam and hot surface hazard analysis within food manufacturing environments, establishing robust plant operation documentation.

// Overview

Conducted within a food-grade manufacturing facility, this study performed a systematic HAZOP (Hazard and Operability Study) on steam distribution lines and high-temperature surface zones. The objective was to document existing hazards, assess risk levels, and propose mitigation measures aligned with HSE standards.

// Methodology

Each node of the steam system was examined using structured guidewords (No, More, Less, Reverse, Other). Temperature mapping was performed using thermal imaging. Findings were cross-referenced with OSHA 29 CFR 1910 and local HSE regulations to assign risk ratings.

// Results

Identified 14 high-priority hazard nodes, 9 of which required immediate engineering controls. Proposed insulation retrofits for 6 steam lines and developed a lockout/tagout (LOTO) protocol for maintenance activities in restricted zones.

// Conclusion

The HAZOP exercise produced a comprehensive safety dossier now in use by the facility's HSE department. Implementing the recommended controls is projected to reduce incident probability in the identified zones by over 60%.
